page: Microsoft Azure D32s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 128 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It has no local storage bundled, though it is Premium Storage capable, and contains no GPUs.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance in specific workloads. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression reach top-tier levels at 36.79 GB/sec and 15.35 GB/sec respectively. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and PassMark CPU physics tests score in the strong category, while stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and database operations fall in the average range. Memory bandwidth tests show 2687.3 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 165.91 GB/sec for uncached large blocks.

The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which assist in vector and matrix operations. This is reflected in strong prompt processing speeds for small and medium LLM models. However, text generation for large 70B models is weak at 2.77 tokens/sec due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration. This server is suited for general-purpose workloads, web serving, and CPU-bound data processing tasks that benefit from high clock speeds and advanced instruction sets, but is less optimal for large-scale LLM text generation.
description: The Microsoft Azure D32s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dsv7 family, powered by a 32-vCPU Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 128 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core, but includes no local storage or GPUs. Performance benchmarks show top-tier single-threaded compression and decompression speeds, alongside strong static web serving throughput and PassMark CPU scores. The processor supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, which enhance vector and matrix operations. While prompt processing for small and medium language models is strong, text generation for large models is limited by the lack of hardware acceleration. This instance is suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, and CPU-bound data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs at 4.2 GHz, 128 GB of RAM, and support for advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32s_v7 is a general-purpose VM with 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and support for AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring high-frequency Intel Xeon processors and advanced matrix extensions for accelerated CPU workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor at 4.2 GHz
128 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core
Top-tier single-threaded compression and decompression benchmark performance
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector and matrix operations
Suited for general-purpose workloads, web serving, and CPU-bound data processing
